segments.text: Lord, you have been our dwelling place throughout all generations.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Before the mountains were born or you brought forth the whole world, from everlasting to everlasting you are God.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You turn people back to dust, saying, 'Return to dust, you mortals.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: A thousand years in your sight are like a day that has just gone by, or like a watch in the night.
Yet you sweep people away in the sleep of death— they are like the new grass of the morning:
In the morning it springs up new, but by evening it is dry and withered.
We are consumed by your anger and terrified by your indignation.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: You have set our iniquities before you, our secret sins in the light of your presence.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: All our days pass away under your wrath; we finish our years with a moan.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Our days may come to seventy years, or eighty, if our strength endures; yet the best of them are but trouble and sorrow, for they quickly pass, and we fly away.
If only we knew the power of your anger! Your wrath is as great as the fear that is your due.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Relent, Lord! How long will it be? Have compassion on your servants.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Satisfy us in the morning with your unfailing love, that we may sing for joy and be glad all our days.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Make us glad for as many days as you have afflicted us, for as many years as we have seen trouble.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: May your deeds be shown to your servants, your splendor to their children.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: May the favor of the Lord our God rest on us; establish the work of our hands for us— yes, establish the work of our hands.
segments.speaker: Moses
